title: My Loneliness Is Real and Not My Fault
subtitle: An affirmation for adult loneliness and the shame that tends to come with it
excerpt: A short affirmation for adult loneliness. Rejects both the idea that loneliness is self-inflicted and the idea that it should be hidden. Names it as a real experience that doesn't carry the moral weight culture tends to attach to it.

practice.whenToUse: Use when adult loneliness is present and you are carrying it as a personal failing or something to be ashamed of.

content.text: A short affirmation for adult loneliness. The cultural framing of loneliness as something you bring on yourself, or as evidence of being unlikeable or deficient, is not accurate. Adult friendship is structurally difficult. Life circumstances are often isolating. Loneliness is a common experience that carries more shame than it deserves.
Say it when the loneliness is present. Three times, at speaking pace.
My loneliness is real. It is not my fault. I am not required to hide it.
Three sentences. The first names the experience as real. The second removes the moral weight. The third removes the obligation to perform being fine. All three are necessary.
